Title: Characterization of a tomato mis-sense mutant chlh reveals a new function of ChlH in fruit ripening

Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Bulked-segregant resequencing/BSA: BioProject PRJNA1108922; RNA-seq: GEO GSE267238
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant mapping; CAPS co-segregation; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; genomic complementation with native promoter; RT-qPCR; immunoblot; metabolite profiling; TEM plastid ultrastructure; ethylene and carotenoid measurements
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during fruit ripening from 35 dpa, peaks at 41 dpa, then declines; also expressed in leaves/seedlings
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ethylene drives climacteric fruit ripening; carotenoids contribute to fruit pigmentation and nutritional quality
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced chlorophyll, cold-sensitive/albino phenotype in stronger alleles, seedling lethality in frameshift mutants, reduced fruit weight and soluble solids, delayed ripening
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes fruit ripening, ethylene biosynthesis, carotenoid accumulation, plastid conversion, and plastid retrograde signaling; mut26 allele delays ripening and lowers ethylene/carotenoids
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlChlH was identified as the causal gene of mut26 via BSA/CAPS and complementation. 2. The P398S missense allele confers a weak gun phenotype and reduced tetrapyrrole synthesis. 3. SlChlH positively regulates tomato fruit ripening by promoting ethylene emission, carotenoid accumulation, and chloroplast-to-chromoplast transition.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This study emphasizes a new noncanonical role of ChlH/GUN5 in ripening through plastid retrograde signaling rather than only chlorophyll biosynthesis. The causal lesion is Pro398Ser and appears weak/viable, whereas frameshift alleles are albino lethal.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural greenhouse ripening conditions; norflurazon treatment for gun phenotype assay; low temperature enhances chlorophyll-deficient phenotype
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune ripening timing, firmness, and carotenoid content, but strong loss-of-function is not usable due to lethality and chlorophyll defects; weak alleles may be useful for shelf-life manipulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic crossing to generate mut26 Slhy5 double mutant; RT-qPCR of SlPhANGs under norflurazon treatment; carotenoid profiling; ethylene measurement; ripening-stage phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transiently and partially induced during ripening in a GUN5-dependent manner
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently induced during fruit ripening; active in seedlings for PhANG regulation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Unknown cofactor Y; HSP90 (proposed pathway context)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Plastid retrograde signaling-linked regulation of photosynthesis-associated nuclear genes; minor effect on carotenoid accumulation/fruit coloration
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slhy5 shows delayed fruit coloration and reduced β-carotene/lycopene; long hypocotyl; double mutant with mut26 is severely albino and infertile/poor fruit set
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes SlPhANG expression downstream of GUN5 in seedlings; no major effect on ACS2, ACO1, PSY1, RIN, TAGL1, or FUL1 expression at matched MG/BR stages in single mutant
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts downstream of GUN5 in plastid retrograde signaling for SlPhANG expression, but is dispensable for normal ethylene production and overall ripening progression in single mutant
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlHY5 expression is partially induced by GUN5 during ripening. 2. In norflurazon-treated seedlings, SlHY5 is epistatic to GUN5 for regulation of SlPhANG expression. 3. Slhy5 fruits show slower coloration and reduced carotenoids but near-WT ethylene emission and ripening timing when staged properly.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carotenoids contribute to fruit pigmentation and nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GUN5-mediated plastid retrograde signaling during ripening; norflurazon-sensitive plastid status
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent downstream component of GUN5-mediated retrograde signaling rather than decisive master regulator of ripening
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper argues that SlHY5 affects coloration more than ripening progression. Prior reports showing stronger ripening effects may reflect fruit sampling by age instead of developmental stage.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Norflurazon treatment in seedlings; natural ripening stages MG/BR/BR+3/BR+6
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely acts with unknown cofactor Y; HSP90 proposed in pathway context but not directly validated here
